feed.title: SAP Community - SD (Sales and Distribution)
feed.subtitle: SD (Sales and Distribution) Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Need support on batch printing packing list in ERP/ EWM
entry.content:
Currently, our system is configured so, that Packing list ZPL0 Messagetype is sent from EWM as PPF action once the packing in SCWM/PACK is completed. ERP then creates a packing list and uses Batchuser to print it with a specific printer.

We have multiple work centers in EWM which pack the goods but it is printing only from one printer because it is configured like that in ERP, but we need to configure the Workcenter in ERP or EWM in such a way, that the print of packing list should be mapped to a workplace in ERP or in EWM so that the print should be done from the same workcenter in SCWM /PACK  ?

 

How is it possible?

entry.title: MASTER CONTRACT IN SALES CONTRACT
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Could you please explain the purpose and functionality of the Master Contract field in a Sales Contract?

Specifically, I would like to understand:

  • What is the business use of the Master Contract field?

  • Is it possible to link or reference multiple sales contracts under a single Master Contract?

  • Can these linked contracts have different customers and different materials?

  • How does this functionality help from a process and reporting perspective?

Kindly guide me through the process flow and any key configuration or practical considerations involved.

I have attached a screenshot for reference.

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: Creating a customer returns (CBAR) with reference to Sales scheduling agreement (LZ)
entry.content:
Is it possible to create a customer returns (CBAR) with reference to a sales scheduling agreement with document type (LZ)?

i heard that the only options available are Sales order, solution order and invoice.

I also tried copying control from LZ to CBAR. But, this config is not editable (there is no option for new entries)
Config name : "Set Copying Control: Sales Document to Sales Document"

Are there any other configs or ways available to achieve this functionality????

entry.title: UoM changed in Va02 not reflecting in MB51
entry.content:
Hi,

A sales order is created in VA01 with order quantity in bottles. After saving the order, the entry is showing in MB51.  Then the user goes to VA02 to change the unit from bottles to Cases. After saving the order when checking MB51, the new units (CS) and the converted quantity not showing in the list of MB51. 

When debugged, I could see that MSEG table is not hit for any updates during the process and I can see that MB51 fields are coming from MSEG.

I would like to understand if this is expected behavior or if there is anything I am missing. If I cannot see the changes in MB51, where should I check?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Billing document not showing in e-document cockpit for CC India & Switzerland customer - S4HPC
entry.content:
S/4 HANA Public Cloud: I have posted a billing document XXXX for company code INDIA and Customer from Switzerland has a VAT number in identification tab and all the necessary configuration has been created in the system, but still no e-document is generated in e-document cockpit. For other country customers like Hong Kong it is working fine.

Can you please suggest what could be the issue with CH customer ?

entry.title: MANAGE SERVICE QUOTATION
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

What is the purpose of Manage Service Quotations when the service sales process can already be handled using Manage Sales Quotations? I would like to understand the specific functionalities, options, and use cases that differentiate Manage Service Quotations from Manage Sales Quotations.

Additionally, I have tested the process using Manage Service Quotations with a service material and customer. However, the system is throwing errors during processing, which can be seen in the attached image. Kindly review and advise on the possible cause and resolution.

Best regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: Need BAdI for Auto-Setting Validity Date in Sales Enquiries
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement for sales enquiries (document type IN) where:

  1. Auto-Set Validity Date
    • When the enquiry is saved, if the Validity Date is empty, it should be automatically set to 30 days from the Enquiry Date.
  2. Freeze Item Quantity After Validity Expiry
    • If the current date is greater than the Validity Date:
      • User cannot change Item Quantity.
      • Other fields should remain editable.
  3. Restriction
    • This logic should apply only to Enquiries and must not affect Sales Orders or Quotations.

I have tried searching for a BAdI in SE18/SE19 and checked the following standard BAdIs, but none of them seem suitable:

BADI_SD_SALES, BADI_SD_SALES_BASIC,  BADI_SD_CM, BADI_LAYER, ADDR_PRINTFORM_SHORT, BADI_SD_SALES_ITEM, CHECK_COBL_ADDON, EHS_DG_002, ATP_PUBLISH_RESULTS, BADI_SD_TO_FM, BADI_ISPS_GRANT, DIP_UPDATE_FKDAT

Could anyone suggest which BAdI or enhancement point I can use to implement this requirement for sales enquiries only?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Changeable and not changeable item category in a sales order after outbound delivery creation
entry.content:
Hello,

I am observing the following and looking for an explanation for it. Created a sales order with three line items, created an outbound delivery only for the first line item, the quantities for the other two line items are not confirmed. When reopening the sales order in change mode I want to change the item categories for the other two line items, what I see is the following, the delivery priority for the second and third  line item is editable, but the item category is only editable for the second and not for the third line item, what causes this mixed behaviour, the item category is TAN for all three items and is set to be changed with TAB if required (system S4/HANA).

Thank you!

entry.title: How to restrict Billing types (FKART) in transaction VF01 dropdown list.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

How to restrict Billing types (FKART) in transaction VF01 dropdown list and display only required billing types in dropdown list to select by users while creating billing document.

How to achieve this requirement?

 Please suggest the solution.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Alerts for BOP job failure
entry.content:
Hi Dear experts 

We need your input on setting up email alerts for BOP jobs.

In our S/4 system, we run Sales Order ATP using a scheduled BOP job created through the Schedule BOP Run app. Occasionally, these BOP jobs fail for different reasons, but no failure alerts are being triggered. We discussed this with our Basis team, and they concluded that because the job is not created through the SAP GUI and is considered a dynamic job, alerts cannot be configured.

Given that this is a business‑critical job, we would like your guidance on whether there is any way to receive alerts when the BOP job fails.

entry.title: common number range objects in ECC Vs S/4Hana
entry.content:
1. Whether below number range objects in ECC are still valid in S/4Hana or not?

NACH (Output conditions) 

KONH (Master conditions) 

KONV (Document conditions) --> As table KONV is replaced with PRCD_ELEMENTS in S/4Hana so KNUMH (cond. record number) will be generated via KONV number range object only or some other mechanism?

2. Can we override above number range objects to have specific custom number range rather than using standard 01 number range object controlled by SAP?

entry.title: sap sd ticket
entry.content: To split delivery based on Transportation group :- There is a need to split the deliverybased on Transportation Group assigned in Material Master. When creating a Sales Order of two materialswith different Transportation Groups, system should split the delivery. How can we configure this scenario ?

entry.title: there is no linkage between sales order and STO PO number for customizing report named Demand Report
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Hope you are doing well.

We are running MRP in a Make-to-Order (MTO) scenario where a Sales Order triggers MRP, resulting in Planned Orders and Purchase Requisitions, which are later converted into STO Purchase Orders. We need to develop a demand tracking report to trace the end-to-end linkage Sales Order → Purchase Requisition → STO Purchase Order; however, we could not find any standard SAP S/4HANA CDS View that provides this consolidated linkage, as available CDS views are module-specific and do not support cross-process traceabil

ity for MTO scenarios. Please provide the solution.

As per below screenshot, there are 4 fields in demand report which we have unable to find the CDS view to get data of below 4 fields

1. STO PO

2. STO PO DATE

3. STO PLANT

4. PLAN ORDER. 

Regards,

entry.title: Going from gATP to AATP, is there an equivalent for EDQA in BOP?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have made the move going from gATP to AATP. We created the BOP run etc but we are still missing EDQA (event-driven quantity assignment). Currently we run the BOP very frequently to replicate EDQA however, this results in sales documents being locked frequently (for one second). 

Is there any way to replicate EDQA in AATP without having to run BOP every x minutes? I have looked at the roadmap but I haven't seen any mention for this yet.

Thanks a lot already!

entry.title: Cash discount indicator is automatically set in Material master SAP public cloud
entry.content:
In SAP Public cloud edition,
I see that in the while creating a material master through Fiori apps "Manage product master data", "Create material - MM01" and through migration cockpit also,

I'm seeing the cash discount indicator in Sales org 1 view is automatically set.
I can see that in SAP on-premise and cloud private editions, it was mentioned as an satndard behavior. (See SAP note 1813559).

Is this an standard behavior in SAP public cloud edition also?????

entry.title: Delivery report for Sales Scheduling agreements
entry.content:
Hi.

I have a requirement to generate a report that lists all outbound deliveries created for a specific Sales Scheduling Agreement in SAP public cloud.

The standard document flow within the Sales Scheduling Agreement does not meet the business requirement, as a different reporting format is needed. I have also tried using transaction VL06O, but there is no available option to filter by the Sales Scheduling Agreement number.

Requirements:

  1. A list/report of outbound deliveries created for a particular Sales Scheduling Agreement.

  2. The ability to filter the report by the Sales Scheduling Agreement number.

Could you please suggest any alternative standard reports, transactions, or approaches to achieve this?

entry.title: "Schedule Billing Creation" Batch job collective billing
entry.content:
Hi.

We are currently using the background job “Schedule Billing Creation” to automatically create billing documents for Sales Scheduling Agreements.

As part of this process, the job creates billing documents for outbound deliveries that have been goods issue posted and are available in the Billing Due List.

However, we are facing an issue where the batch job is combining all outbound deliveries related to a single Sales Scheduling Agreement into one invoice, with each outbound delivery appearing as a line item on the same invoice. This follows the concept of collective billing.

Our requirement is to prevent this behavior. We would like the batch job to create a separate invoice for each outbound delivery.

Could you please advise if there is any configuration available to ensure that this job generates only one invoice per outbound delivery?

I have updated the Billing Settings in the VF04 - Create Billing Documents Fiori app to create separate billing documents for each item in billing due list. But, still it's not working.

entry.title: How to change bank account on the invoice form in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a problem with the bank account on the invoice output. The invoice output only shows one specific bank account, even if this account is deactivated in the Manage Bank Accounts app. I set up a different bank account number in the same app that I want to show on the invoice form, but the change didn't show up in the output.
Could you please clarify where exactly I can specify or activate the bank account to be used for the invoice? Alternatively, what settings are required for this?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: block Purchase requisitions creation when there is a delivery block active on sales order
entry.content:
Hello, 

We are using a delivery block on Sales orders received via EDI where we activated the purchase requisition block. However, when the mrp is running it keeps creating purchase requisitions. 

Is there a possibility to block PR creation for normal sales orders until all checks are done? 

I found out OSS note 738171 but it seems it is concerning item category TAS en TAB, thus not sure if this is relevant here.

Kr,

entry.title: OData V4 URL Encoding Issue with SAP Web Dispatcher + Proxy (Sales Order Management App)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with OData V4 URL encoding after activating the standard Sales Order Management application in S/4HANA.

Note: An SAP incident has already been created, and in parallel we are reaching out to the community to learn from experts who may have faced a similar issue.

Issue summary

The problem occurs because equal signs (=) in the OData V4 request URL are being URL-encoded (%3D) before the request reaches the S/4HANA ICM.

In the browser, the request URL is correct and the = signs are not encoded:

https://etgwdsp.etgworld.com/sap/opu/odata4/sap/c_salesordermanage_srv/srvd_f4/sap/c_orgdivisionvaluehelp/0001;ps='srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va='com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

However, when the request reaches the ICM, the raw HTTP request shows that:

  • = is encoded as %3D

  • %27 is converted back to '

Example from ICM level 3 trace:

GET /sap/opu/odata4/.../0001;ps%3D'srvd-c_salesordermanage_sd-0001';va%3D'com.sap.gateway.srvd.c_salesordermanage_sd.v0001.ae-c_salesordermanage.createwithsalesordertype.organizationdivision.SalesOrderManageType.X'/$metadata?sap-language=EN

The SAP Gateway expects the “=” characters to remain unencoded, and because of this encoding, the request fails.

Landscape and behavior

Middleware involved

  • SAP Web Dispatcher

  • Corporate HTTP Proxy

Observed behavior

  1. All apps work when the proxy is bypassed (Web Dispatcher active)

  2. All apps work when the Web Dispatcher is bypassed (proxy active)

  3. The issue occurs only when both proxy and Web Dispatcher are active

This strongly indicates that the URL is being modified due to an interaction between the proxy and Web Dispatcher.

Troubleshooting performed

  • All required roles, authorizations, services, and ICF nodes are active

  • Web Dispatcher profile parameters adjusted to prevent URL encoding

  • Web Dispatcher mod file changes attempted for OData V4 handling
    → Issue still persists

As per internal network team analysis:

  • A change may be required in the Web Dispatcher index file

  • Neither the network team nor BASIS team has access to modify it

  • Only SAP can make changes at this level

Questions to the community

  • Has anyone experienced a similar OData V4 URL encoding issue with Web Dispatcher + proxy?

  • Is this a known limitation or defect in SAP Web Dispatcher for OData V4?

  • Are there any supported parameters, SAP Notes, or workarounds to prevent encoding of = in the URL?

  • Should this be handled via ICM settings, Web Dispatcher configuration, or proxy rules?

  • Is there any recommended architectural workaround until SAP provides a fix?

Any guidance, experiences, or references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Varsha J S

 

#SAP #S4HANA #ODataV4 #SAPGateway #SAPWebDispatcher #ICM #SAPBasis #SAPFiori #SAPCommunity
@SAP @SAPCommunity @SAPSupport
